hawkish_phrases: voted unanimously to increase Bank Rate by 0.25 percentage points, to 0.75%
a small margin of excess demand emerges by late 2019 and builds thereafter
an ongoing tightening of monetary policy over the forecast period would be appropriate
unit labour cost growth has firmed
higher growth in domestic costs than has been seen over recent years
dovish_phrases: Any future increases in Bank Rate are likely to be at a gradual pace and to a limited extent
consumption grows in line with the subdued pace of real incomes
the projected pace of GDP growth over the forecast is slightly faster than the diminished rate of supply growth
summary: The MPC voted unanimously 9-0 to raise Bank Rate by 25bp to 0.75%, with explicit guidance that ongoing tightening over the forecast period would be appropriate to return inflation sustainably to target, though future increases would be gradual and limited.
